---
title: "Projects"
description: "Research projects and directions."
---

## Current projects

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card}
### Individualised niches and adaptive rewiring
A theoretical ecology direction studying when species-level dynamic networks are valid reductions of individual-level niche dynamics, and when hidden individual variation makes graph-level rewiring biologically insufficient.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card}
### Cross-scale molecular communication
A mathematical approach to molecular and nano-biological communication where end-to-end signalling feasibility depends on active interfaces and multi-level realizability.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card}
### Evolving communication networks
Geometric diagnostics for network reconfiguration, boundary-conditioned feasibility, interface loops, and obstruction-based no-go certificates.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card}
### Biological organization and circular causality
Mathematical tools for studying circular causality, operational closure, and non-homogeneous lifted recurrence in multi-level biological systems.
:::
:::
:::

## Future directions

- concrete simulations of individual-generated ecological networks;
- algorithmic diagnostics for evolving network feasibility;
- biological case studies of active interfaces and structural reconfiguration;
- simplified examples for teaching local-to-global obstruction ideas to interdisciplinary audiences.
